content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Both amendments identify real defects verified against the code. fr-15-1d44cf39: execute_iterative_plan_and_implement_stage computes recovery_target from persisted stage_target (engine.rs:7072) but only uses it for terminal-resume detection (7087); the actual next invocation uses resolved_target.clone() (7160), and resume preflight (2333) also uses stage_plan[].target — so a mid-stage config/backend change can cause resumed iterations to run on a different backend than the one recorded in the persisted iterative state. fr-15-bbad6dde: The Codex ResponseDecoder reads parsed_payload from a temporary last-message file that is cleaned up after success (process_backend.rs:306-386), keeping only stdout as raw output. Iterative sidecar persistence is only best-effort (engine.rs:6345-6379), and the raw-output fallback recover_structured_payload_from_process_stdout treats non-Claude stdout as plain JSON (process_backend.rs:1925-1933), which Codex stdout typically is not. A crash between a successful Codex iteration and the stage commit, combined with a failed sidecar write, can leave the iteration unrecoverable on resume.

## Votes

- `fr-15-1d44cf39`: Accept
  Verified: iterative_resume_target (engine.rs:6199-6221) produces recovery_target from the persisted stage_target, but execute_iterative_plan_and_implement_stage passes resolved_target.clone() (not recovery_target) into invoke_stage_on_backend at line 7160 for subsequent iterations, and resume_run preflights stage_plan[resume_state.stage_index].target (line 2333) instead of the persisted target. This means any change to workflow.implementer_backend/model between crash and resume silently switches backends mid-stage or fails preflight even though the prior iterations ran under the original target. The remediation hint correctly points at honoring the persisted stage_target for the remainder of the stage attempt.
- `fr-15-bbad6dde`: Accept
  Verified: Codex decoder reads the structured payload from message_path and stores only stdout as RawOutputReference::Inline (process_backend.rs:289, 397), with best_effort_cleanup deleting the last-message file (line 386). persist_invocation_parsed_payload_best_effort only logs on failure (engine.rs:6345-6379), so the sidecar can be absent. recover_iterative_payload_from_raw_output falls back to recover_structured_payload_from_process_stdout, which for non-Claude backends naively does serde_json::from_str(raw_output) (process_backend.rs:1930-1932) on Codex stdout — typically a transcript, not pure JSON. A crash after a successful Codex iteration but before stage commit, with a failed best-effort sidecar write, leaves the iteration unrecoverable despite having succeeded. The remediation (mandatory sidecar persistence, or retaining the real last-message payload) is well-targeted.
